name: return_at_exact_boundary_increments
description: current_return exactly equals (dynamic_activation - DEADBAND). The producer uses `>=` so this fires the increment branch. Pins the inclusive boundary.
derivation: dynamic_activation = max(0.4, min(3.0, 1.0)) = 1.0. threshold = 1.0 - 0.2 = 0.8. current_return (0.8) >= 0.8 (EXACT equality) -> ticks++ -> 2 + 1 = 3. 3 < 5 -> not locked. not locked -> stop = base_stop_level = 0.2. Catches an impl using strict `>` (would reset at boundary instead of incrementing).
